feed.title: Daily Digest
feed.subtitle: Quote, fact, life hack, fortune cookie, and headlines of the day

entry.title: USA vs. Paraguay live score, result, highlights from USMNT's 2026 World Cup Group D match
entry.content: The Sporting News is tracking live scoring updates and highlights for USA vs. Paraguay in the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Follow for complete results from the Group D match in Los Angeles.

entry.title: World Cup 2026 opening ceremony live updates, reviews on Katy Perry and other performances in USA
entry.content: The Sporting News is tracking live updates and reviews from Katy Perry and every performance during the 2026 World Cup opening ceremony in Inglewood.

entry.title: Ole Miss vs. North Carolina baseball box score: Live stats from College World Series
entry.content: The Tar Heels and Rebels meet for the first time in 57 years.

entry.title: USA vs. Paraguay box score: Full stats from World Cup Group D match for USMNT
entry.content: The United States gets its World Cup underway on Friday night.

entry.title: Is Carli Lloyd is pregnant? News on former USWNT star and current analyst's expanding family
entry.content: Here's the latest on Carli Lloyd's second pregnancy and her growing family.

entry.title: Two returning Panthers facing tough NFL training camp challenges
entry.content: With OTAs ending Thursday, TE James Mitchell and DL LaBryan Ray could be pressured to keep their roster spots.

entry.title: Klay Thompson's days in Dallas are numbered, and these 3 teams should be calling
entry.content: Klay Thompson’s time in Dallas is likely over after a chaotic roster reset, but these three NBA teams could still use his legendary championship shooting.

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
Stay healthy. Walk a mile.
Lucky Numbers: 46, 53, 41, 36, 51, 98

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: Wrap your bananas in tin foil to make them last longer.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: Fish that live more than 800 meters below the ocean surface don't have eyes.

entry.title: Quote of the Day — Henry Ford
entry.content: “You can't build a reputation on what you are going to do.” — Henry Ford

entry.title: 2026 World Cup of Darts: Draw, schedule, prize money and format explained
entry.content: One of the best tournaments on the darts calendar is almost here!

entry.title: Alabama seeks to execute man by lethal injection
entry.content: Alabama is seeking to execute a man with lethal injection hours after his nitrogen execution was prevented from going forward

entry.title: Kids' nonprofit in Seattle returns 20 free World Cup tickets over barring referee
entry.content: A youth-soccer nonprofit in Seattle has returned 20 free World Cup tickets to protest the U.S. decision to bar Somali referee Omar Artan from entering the country

entry.title: US Democratic lawmakers pledge to help speed up disaster recovery in Puerto Rico
entry.content: A group of U.S. Democratic lawmakers have promised Puerto Ricans that they would try to speed up the island’s sluggish recovery from destructive hurricanes and earthquakes, a process that relies heavily on federal funds

entry.title: Taiwan's opposition leader touts talks with China as necessary for peace
entry.content: Taiwan’s Beijing-friendly opposition leader says her party is committed to defense of the self-ruled island that China views as its own but that peace is possible by taking permanent secession off the table

entry.title: Treasury expands bank data-sharing rules tied to Trump immigration crackdown
entry.content: The Treasury Department has moved to involve banks more deeply in President Donald Trump’s immigration crackdown

entry.title: Bystander wounded in shooting near White House still undergoing treatment
entry.content: A bystander wounded during an exchange of gunfire near the White House last month is an active-duty soldier and is still being treated for his wounds, according to the law firm he has retained

entry.title: New UFO files describe spinning discs, glowing orbs
entry.content: New UFO files have been released by the Trump administration that include several vivid descriptions of mysterious sightings in the sky

entry.title: Blood test can find thousands of genetic conditions in pregnancy, say scientists
entry.content: Technique that examines fragments of foetal DNA in mother’s bloodstream could limit need for invasive screening, according to researchersA new maternal blood test that can detect thousands of serious genetic conditions in the developing foetus could limit the need for invasive screening during pregnancy, according to scientists.The test, to be described at the European Society for Human Genetics conference in Gothenburg on Saturday, relies on detecting tiny fragments of a foetus’s DNA that circulate in the mother’s bloodstream during pregnancy. Using advanced sequencing techniques, scientists were able to identify a very high proportion of genetic conditions, such as cystic fibrosis, that are currently only reliably diagnosed using amniocentesis or other invasive tests. Continue reading...

entry.title: Merope Mills awarded CBE in king’s honours list for Martha’s rule campaign
entry.content: Journalist and healthcare campaigner was driving force behind patient safety initiative after death of 13-year-old daughterThe healthcare campaigner and journalist Merope Mills has been made a CBE in the king’s birthday honours list for services to patient safety.Mills, a senior editor at the Guardian, was a driving force behind the introduction of an initiative in England said to have potentially saved hundreds of lives. She has spent years campaigning for the introduction of Martha’s rule under which patients, relatives and staff can seek a second opinion if they have concerns about the care being provided. Continue reading...

entry.title: One Ukrainian City’s Agonizing Year as the Russian Army Closed In
entry.content: Over the course of a year, the Journal followed Ukrainians in a town on the front line as the fabric of their lives was slowly, painfully torn apart.

entry.title: Severe storms, tornadoes slam Midwest, killing at least 1
entry.content: Severe storms that swept through the Midwest late Thursday knocked out power to hundreds of thousands of customers, damaged buildings and canceled flights.

entry.title: At least 1 killed, 9 hospitalized in Texas mass shooting; suspect dead
entry.content: The suspect, Victor Mata Villarreal, was wanted for attempted murder of a police officer after shooting at law enforcement during a vehicle chase earlier this week, officials said.

entry.title: Who is Trump's new nominee for director of national intelligence, Jay Clayton?
entry.content: Former Securities and Exchange Commission chairman Jay Clayton has been nominated to be the next director of national intelligence by President Trump. CBS News' Olivia Gazis reports.

entry.title: "48 Hours:" Case By Case: The biggest cases, explained as they unfold
entry.content: The cases everyone's talking about, explained as they happen. A weekly update on the biggest criminal stories unfolding now.

entry.title: Trump nominates Jay Clayton to lead intelligence after Pulte backlash
entry.content: Jay Clayton, the former SEC chair, is President Trump's latest nominee for director of national intelligence. Plus, the Pentagon is sued by The New York Times for "Orwellian" press restrictions, and a judge rules that this weekend's UFC fight at the White House may go forward. CBS News legal reporter Katrina Kaufman has the latest.

entry.title: Watch: Pentagon releases 6 new UFO videos
entry.content: The Defense Department released a third batch of UFO files on Friday, three weeks after its second drop. These are all of the videos in the latest tranche, plus analysis from astrophysicist Avi Loeb.

entry.title: Victor Wembanyama on Spurs' belief they can still win title down 3-1: 'Everyone knows we're gonna do it'
entry.content: Only one team in NBA Finals history has ever emerged as champs after falling into a 3-1 hole.

entry.title: Belgium midfielder Witsel 'not staying at Girona' and will not return to Standard Liège
entry.content: While preparing for Belgium's World Cup opener against Egypt, midfielder Axel Witsel said he has no intention of returning to Girona after one season with the La Liga club. The 37-year-old Witsel joined Girona after three years at Atlético Madrid but because Girona was relegated to the second division last month he was not interest in remaining with the team past June 30, when his contract expires. “I am not staying at Girona because I am a free agent and we have been relegated," Witsel said in French on Friday.

entry.title: Palestinian FA chief not yet given visa for US to watch World Cup
entry.content: Palestinian football chief Jibril Rajoub cannot yet enter the United States or Canada for the men's World Cup because he has not been granted a visa. "To this day, I have received neither a visa to enter the United States nor Canada.

entry.title: 📸 Nearly five years out! Cast-off shocks World Cup hosts
entry.content: Stories that only football can write. Or rather: that only the 2026 World Cup can write. In Canada, Bosnia and Herzegovina just took the lead through a striker who had actually long since been discard...

entry.title: €20m Liverpool ace in demand as Champions League club seek deal
entry.content: Liverpool's summer transfer window is going to be one of the busiest in the Premier League again this time around. In the 2025-26 season, the Reds earned over £200 million in transfer fees while spen...

entry.title: Empty seats at Canada’s opening World Cup match after ticket price controversy
entry.content: It comes after there were thousands of empty seats in just the second match of the tournament between South Africa and Czech Republic on Thursday evening

entry.title: Ryan Garcia vs. Conor Benn: Golden Boy issues Zuffa Boxing with cease-and-desist
entry.content: The negotiations for Ryan Garcia vs. Conor Benn have hit a major roadblock.

entry.title: Big inning lifts Muskego past Appleton North in state softball semifinal
entry.content: Muskego gets key three-run home run in 6-2 victory over Appleton North in WIAA Division 1 state softball semifinal game in Madison.

entry.title: Good Samaritan New Yorker speaks out after decrying vandalizing following Knicks game
entry.content: Balladoli Miese, a New Yorker from Puerto Rico, calmed a rowdy crowd and asked them to avoid damaging public property after a Knicks loss on Monday. "It's our city, we should not be breaking our city," Miese said. CBS News' Jared Ochacher reports.

entry.title: Deal With Iran Is Close, but Accord Will Kickstart Toughest Phase of Talks
entry.content: U.S., Iranian and Pakistani negotiators say an agreement will begin dialogue over Tehran’s nuclear program.

entry.title: Turf wars: Why the World Cup is a ‘slap in the face’ to NFL players
entry.content: When the United States men’s national team takes the pitch for its first World Cup match, its players’ feet will land on newly installed grass at SoFi Stadium in Inglewood, California, typically the home of the NFL’s Los Angeles Rams and Los Angeles Chargers.

entry.title: Notable Deaths in 2026
entry.content: A look back at the esteemed personalities who've left us this year, who'd touched us with their innovation, creativity and humanity.

entry.title: What would it take to stop women from bleeding to death after childbirth?
entry.content: A newly published series of reports calls attention to a dire situation facing millions of women after childbirth — and the solutions that can prevent death from postpartum hemorrhage.

entry.title: Elon Musk becomes first trillionaire after SpaceX IPO
entry.content: Elon Musk became the world's first trillionaire after his company SpaceX began trading publicly. SpaceX is the first of what is expected to be many major IPOs related to AI.

entry.title: Ohio voting rights group facing criminal fraud investigation, sources say
entry.content: The FBI executed a search warrant at the office of an Ohio-based group known for its voter registration work as part of an ongoing fraud-related investigation, sources told CBS News.

entry.title: Carlos Bocanegra on "intense" 2026 FIFA World Cup U.S.-Paraguay match
entry.content: Former U.S. Men's National Team captain Carlos Bocanegra made sports history, playing in two past World Cup tournaments. Bocanegra shares successful routine tips for players and has more on what fans can expect from the U.S.-Paraguay match.

entry.title: Bond Investor Ken Leech Pleads Guilty to Obstructing SEC Probe
entry.content: Prosecutors accused the former chief investment officer of Western Asset Management of leading a cherry-picking fraud scheme.

entry.title: Backstreet Boys reveal music video for song from new "Paw Patrol" movie
entry.content: The Backstreet Boys join "CBS Mornings" to reveal the music video for their song, "Bottle Up," from "Paw Patrol: The Dino Movie." The band talks about the song and how their kids reacted to finding out they would be part of the new film.

entry.title: Musk becomes world's first trillionaire, at least on paper, as SpaceX begins trading
entry.content: Elon Musk crossed the threshold to become the world's first trillionaire Friday -- at least on paper -- after SpaceX priced its initial public offering at $135 a share, pushing his net worth to just over $1 trillion.

entry.title: How a Gilded-Age Revolution Paved the Way for Trillionaires
entry.content: The 19th century titans did more than build railroads. They paved the way for the reinvention of the stock market—and the creation of runaway wealth.

entry.title: Uproar over planned data center near Nashville Zoo: "We the people have spoken"
entry.content: Residents are protesting plans for a 70,000-square-foot data center near the Nashville Zoo. CBS News' Skyler Henry reports.

entry.title: How SpaceX is influencing the local economy in Texas
entry.content: Since Elon Musk's SpaceX first broke ground on Starbase in 2014, the city of Brownsville, Texas, says its population has grown by more than 10,000 people. Jason Allen looks at how the company has impacted the local economy.

entry.title: Former SpaceX employee talks about impact of company going public
entry.content: A former SpaceX welder, who still holds shares in the company, talks with CBS News about how he got involved in the company and the impact of SpaceX going public. Jo Ling Kent reports.

entry.title: SpaceX cuts retail IPO allocation to low 20% range, source says
entry.content: The Elon Musk-led company plans to direct a percentage in the low 20s of the offering to retail buyers, according to a person familiar with the matter.

entry.title: Facebook and Instagram appear to down
entry.content: Facebook, WhatsApp and Instagram appear to down for many users this morning.

entry.title: FDA authorizes emergency use of drug to fight screwworms in pets
entry.content: Nitenpyram is the first generic animal drug authorized to treat screwworms in dogs and cats, according to federal regulators.

entry.title: A Bosnian song about disillusionment with the American Dream becomes a World Cup banger
entry.content: The opening lyrics couldn’t be plainer: “I am from Bosnia; take me to America.”

entry.title: Shawn Hatosy on playing fan favorite Dr. Jack Abbot on "The Pitt"
entry.content: Actor Shawn Hatosy went from guest star to Emmy Award-winning fan favorite as Dr. Jack Abbot on the hit medical drama "The Pitt." Dr. Abbot "is very calm in the face of chaos and he can handle anything, but he's struggling, as is Robby, and they have to come together," Hatosy tells "CBS Mornings."

entry.title: Trump rejects Iranian media's portrayal of terms he says were agreed to
entry.content: Iran says a deal with the U.S. is not finalized, and Trump says points laid out by Iranian state media are not "the terms that were agreed to."

entry.title: List of Belfast home addresses shared online amid anti-immigration riots
entry.content: Northern Ireland police say they got calls from distressed Belfast residents as a list of home addresses circulated online amid anti-immigration riots.

entry.title: Suspect linked to U.S. consulate shooting on run in Canada, officer killed
entry.content: A manhunt is underway for an "armed and dangerous" suspect linked to a shooting at the U.S. Consulate in Toronto after an officer was fatally shot.

entry.title: China arrests U.S. researcher on suspicion of spying
entry.content: China says U.S. national U Min Zin, founder of a think tank focused on Myanmar, was detained on suspicion of "espionage and endangering China's national security."

entry.title: Goblin shark seen alive in natural deep-sea habitat for the first time
entry.content: The goblin shark had only previously been seen when caught by fishermen and they died shortly afterward.

entry.title: SpaceX IPO: Everything you need to know
entry.content: TechCrunch has followed SpaceX's start, struggles, and successes from the early days. And we're here for what happens next too. This package of SpaceX IPO coverage includes who stands to win (and maybe some who won't), pre-IPO deals, and what's tucked inside its S-1 registration document.

entry.title: SpaceX set to begin trading after raising $75 billion in record IPO
entry.content: Investors will get their first chance to trade shares of Elon Musk's space company after the market opens at 9:30 a.m. ET.

entry.title: Pentagon releases 3rd batch of UFO files: "Are you seeing this?"
entry.content: The Pentagon on Friday released a new group of documents and videos related to UFOs, or UAPs, with 72 more documents, images and recordings.

entry.title: Stowaway with 524 pounds of cocaine, gun found on barge in Caribbean
entry.content: It's the second time this year that authorities detained a stowaway holding a huge stash of drugs in the same harbor.

entry.title: D.C. watchdogs feud about overspending as city lawmakers eye more budget hikes
entry.content: Washington's top municipal finance watchdogs are feuding about perennial agency overspending as the D.C. Council eyes more spending hikes in its annual budget.

entry.title: From startup to $1.8 trillion: The investors who took a chance on SpaceX now reap the rewards
entry.content: With SpaceX seeking an IPO valuation of nearly $1.8 trillion, early bets are poised to generate some of the biggest paper gains in venture capital history.

entry.title: How to see if Netflix is downgrading your picture quality
entry.content: It might involve connecting a keyboard to your TV, which is as fun as it sounds.

entry.title: Engadget Podcast: Making sense of SpaceX's messy IPO (and its messier CEO)
entry.content: The finance world is excited for SpaceX's IPO, but how can you square that with Elon Musk's ongoing racism?

entry.title: Gemini can now adjust your picture settings on Google TV
entry.content: Gemini can - at least on some TCL models - adjust your TV's picture settings.

entry.title: Reds' JJ Bleday gets major trade update that could help Brewers, Guardians
entry.content: Cincinnati could potentially get some value back for this slugger.

entry.title: The S&P 500 already made a big call on SpaceX stock and index fund investors need to know it
entry.content: The S&P 500 index, where trillions in retirement savings are invested, said no to the SpaceX IPO, the biggest in history. Here's what that means for portfolios.

entry.title: David Hockney, whose art celebrated sun-drenched Los Angeles, dead at 88
entry.content: David Hockney, the innovative and prolific British artist who arrived in Los Angeles in 1964, soon celebrating its sun-drenched life and landscapes in colorful, wildly popular paintings, has died.

entry.title: 2026 World Cup of Darts Day 2 live results, scores and group stage standings: Singapore and Lim close out campaign with a win
entry.content: Two full sessions of action will take place to determine which 12 countries advance.

entry.title: Daniel Espino injury comeback could be best story of entire Cleveland Guardians season
entry.content: Espino is a bit of an inspiration for all the adversity he had to overcome on the way to MLB.

entry.title: World Cup 2026 matches today: Updated FIFA fixtures, games schedule for Friday, June 12
entry.content: Mexico got their World Cup off to a winning start on Thursday. The two other co-hosts are in action today.

entry.title: World’s Food Supply Imperiled by Iran War, Fertilizer Manufacturer Fertiglobe Chief Says
entry.content: Failure to get fertilizer supplies moving through the Persian Gulf risks worsening food shortages and triggering higher grain prices that could take time to come down.

entry.title: USA predictions for 2026 World Cup: Projecting USMNT's matches in Group D, knockouts and what success looks like
entry.content: The Sporting News breaks down what success looks like for the USMNT this month, and projects where the opportunities lie and the danger lurks as the tournament progresses on home soil through the summer heat.

entry.title: Svechnikov, Aho strike as Hurricanes top Golden Knights 4-2 to move within a win of the Stanley Cup
entry.content: It all came together in Game 5, with Svechnikov’s short putaway at the post on the power play giving Carolina a 4-1 lead midway through the third period.

entry.title: Satou Sabally’s bench production lifts New York Liberty to victory against Atlanta Dream
entry.content: The Commissioner’s Cup has been a past indicator of the New York Liberty’s future. In 2023, they beat the Las Vegas Aces in the Cup final before losing to them in the WNBA championship series. The following year, they lost to the Minnesota Lynx in the Commissioner’s Cup championship game but won their first WNBA title that fall, beating the Lynx in a five-game series. Thursday’s 104-90 win over the Atlanta Dream might be just another game. Or, with a commanding lead in the Commissioner’s Cup Eas

entry.title: South Korea 2-1 Czechia: Tigers of Asia come from behind in Guadalajara
entry.content: Goals from Hwang In-beom and Oh Hyeon-gyu saw South Korea come from behind to claim a 2-1 win against Czechia in their opening Group A match at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.South Korea were arguably the be...

entry.title: South Korea come from behind to beat Czech Republic - report & reaction
entry.content: Listen to BBC Radio 5 Live commentary, follow text and score updates and track the latest match stats as South Korea take on Czech Republic in Group A of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, at the Estadio Akron in Guadalajara, Mexico.

entry.title: World Cup 2026: South Korea fights back for 2-1 win over Czechia in pivotal opener
entry.content: South Korea took a major step toward the knockout round in its World Cup opener.

entry.title: Chicago Bears’ $5bn move to Indiana a ‘done deal’ claims ESPN’s Adam Schefter
entry.content: Adam Schefter has claimed the Chicago Bears’ proposed move to Hammond, Indiana is no bluff, describing the stadium push as close to a done deal. The Bears’ stadium saga has dragged through Chicago, Arlington Heights, and now Northwest Indiana, but this latest turn feels different.

entry.title: 📹 Thrilling comeback wraps up Group A's opening round; Copa table
entry.content: The 2026 FIFA World Cup, hosted by the United States, Mexico, and Canada, kicked off this Thursday (11).The opening match ended with Mexico beating South Africa 2-0 at the legendary Azteca Stadium.Bes...

entry.title: Norway checks out that other Cup ahead of World Cup opener
entry.content: Norway is training in Greensboro, North Carolina, about 90 minutes from the Carolina Hurricanes' home.

entry.title: Theker just raised $85M to build the factory robot that doesn’t specialize in anything
entry.content: Unlike humanoid robots designed around a fixed form — think Boston Dynamics — Theker's machines are built to be reconfigured.

entry.title: Thai Princess Bajrakitiyabha dies after more than three years in coma
entry.content: Thai Princess Bajrakitiyabha dies after more than three years in coma

entry.title: Alaska election official threatens to disqualify Republican who shares name with Sen. Dan Sullivan
entry.content: A top Alaska elections official has threatened to disqualify from the August primary a U.S. Senate candidate who shares the same name and party affiliation as incumbent Republican Dan Sullivan

entry.title: Jury convicts the brother of Massachusetts attorney general in rideshare sex assaults
entry.content: A jury has convicted the brother of Massachusetts Attorney General Andrea Campbell of sexually assaulting women while posing as a rideshare driver

entry.title: Jeff Bezos’s Prometheus raises $12B to build an ‘artificial general engineer’ for the physical world
entry.content: The new round values the physical AI startup that aims to automate heavy engineering and drug design at $41 billion.

entry.title: Switzerland’s Radical Proposal on Immigration: Cap the Population
entry.content: Its referendum reflects a broader shift that is upending the long-held consensus that open borders are the key to economic growth.

entry.title: Anti-ICE protester sentenced to 30 months in prison for assaulting a federal officer
entry.content: A man accused of assaulting a federal officer during protests at the U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement building in Portland, Oregon, has been sentenced to 30 months in prison

entry.title: How many did points did Caitlin Clark score tonight? Full stats and highlights from Fever vs. Sky
entry.content: The Fever squared off against the Sky at Gainbridge Fieldhouse.

entry.title: South Korea vs. Czechia box score: Full stats from World Cup Group A match
entry.content: Mexico has taken an early lead in Group A with its win over South Africa. One of these teams will try to win and move into a tie for first on the first day of the World Cup.

entry.title: Maple Leafs' Morgan Rielly trade plans take specific shape
entry.content: Toronto still has to figure out what to do with its defenseman and his big contract.

entry.title: Mexico opens historic World Cup with a win
entry.content: Fans packed Mexico City as the largest World Cup ever got underway, with Mexico defeating South Africa in the tournament's opening match. NBC News’ Stephanie Gosk reports.

entry.title: Alaska election official threatens to disqualify Republican who shares name with Sen. Dan Sullivan
entry.content: Alaska election official threatens to disqualify Republican who shares name with Sen. Dan Sullivan

entry.title: Patriots predicted to show interest in Rams $22 million cut candidate
entry.content: The New England Patriots could show interest in a Los Angeles Rams cut candidate who will be a free agent next offseason, but is coming off the best season of his career in 2025.

entry.title: Closest Hart Trophy votes in NHL history: Where Nikita Kucherov's narrow win over Connor McDavid ranks all-time
entry.content: Nikita Kucherov is a now a two-time MVP, by the smallest of margins. Where does the Lightning star's narrow margin of victory rank all-time?
